div.p: Historically, a user could join a user namespace, use setgroups(2) to drop supplementary groups, and then access files that were restricted to those groups. The kernel now enforces a strict state-machine ordering:

ol.li: Setgroups MUST be set to deny to prove the process will not abuse group-dropping for privilege escalation.
GID Mapping is then permitted, allowing the process to assume a new identity within the namespace boundaries.

ul.li: Result: setgroups(2) and init_groups(3) now return EPERM
Requirement: Must be set before unprivileged GID mapping
Constraint: Unprivileged users cannot write gid_map without first disabling setgroups.
Attack Prevented: Dropping group membership to gain file access (e.g., bypassing rwxr-x---).
